message:
chore: close bead ft-e34d9.10.8.2 (compile-time/runtime guards)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -3458,7 +3458,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.5","title":"[asupersync][ops] Secret-safe diagnostics: redaction, privacy, and artifact hygiene gate","description":"Ensure all runtime diagnostics, telemetry, and evidence artifacts preserve triage utility while preventing leakage of secrets, credentials, tokens, and sensitive user content across CLI/robot/watch/worker surfaces.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Deliver full scope for this bead without regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests for happy path, boundary/edge behavior, and explicit failure/error handling.
3. Add deterministic integration/end-to-end scenarios including at least one failure injection and one recovery path.
4. Emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions, outcomes, reason_code/error_code) and persist triage artifacts.
5. Document run commands and evidence locations and wire them into normal validation/CI paths.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Define a versioned sensitive-data taxonomy and redaction policy covering credentials, tokens, secrets, PII-like fields, and high-risk payload fragments across runtime logs/artifacts.\n2. Implement deterministic redaction/scrubbing rules for structured logs, incident bundles, evidence manifests, and test artifacts while preserving correlation and triage value.\n3. Add comprehensive unit tests for redaction engines (positive and adversarial cases), including no-leak assertions, false-positive bounds, and deterministic output guarantees.\n4. Add integration tests across CLI, robot mode, watch daemon, and health/doctor outputs to verify sensitive fields are redacted consistently and safely.\n5. Add end-to-end scripts that inject synthetic secrets/tokens into realistic workflows and prove they never appear unredacted in logs, traces, manifests, or exported evidence bundles.\n6. Structured diagnostics must include explicit redaction metadata (redaction_rule_id, field_class, hash/fingerprint where appropriate, correlation_id) for auditability without exposing raw secrets.\n7. Heavy validation suites run via rch exec -- <command>; closure requires artifact-level evidence proving zero unredacted secret leakage in gated scenarios.","notes":"diagnostic_redaction.rs: 3-layer redaction (secret-pattern, field-policy, privacy-budget). 27 unit tests pass, clippy clean. Commit dd53b606.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:39:10.084229Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-26T08:54:43.994166Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T08:54:43.994114Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["artifacts","asupersync","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","observability","privacy","quality-gate","security","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.5","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.5","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.5","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.5","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.8","title":"[asupersync][track] Dependency eradication and cutover governance","description":"Finish migration by removing forbidden runtime dependencies and executing controlled cutover.

Scope:
- dependency eradication (tokio/smol/async-io residues)
- compile-time/runtime guards against regression
- canary rollout, rollback rehearsal, and final go/no-go

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Remove direct forbidden runtime dependencies from target crates per migration boundary.
2. Add static guards to prevent runtime dependency reintroduction.
3. Execute canary and rollback rehearsals with explicit success criteria.
4. Final go/no-go requires green unit/integration/e2e suites and operational evidence bundle.
5. Closure includes self-contained migration report capturing decisions, tradeoffs, and residual risks.","notes":"This track prevents migration backsliding and guarantees operationally safe landing. The bar is evidence-based closure, not partial code conversion, and every gate must include structured logging/diagnostics artifacts for auditability. Final acceptance is user-centered: CLI/robot/watch/session workflows must show stable latency, reliability, and recovery behavior under cutover conditions. Heavy compile/test/benchmark/soak workloads for this bead must run via rch exec -- <command>, with reproducible command records and artifact paths captured in completion evidence. Require deterministic fixtures/seeds and replayable test evidence so failures are reproducible across environments. Include failure-injection and degraded-path scenarios (timeouts/cancellation/error classes) with explicit assertions and diagnostics. This bead must include comprehensive unit tests for scope-local logic, edge cases, and failure branches with deterministic fixtures and replayable seeds. This bead must include cross-module integration tests that validate boundary behavior, data contracts, and degraded/failure interactions under representative workloads. All diagnostics/logging/artifact outputs in this bead must comply with secret-safe redaction and privacy policy gates defined in ft-e34d9.10.7.5, with evidence captured in validation artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:24:39.230023Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-12T00:08:37.675272+00:00","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","cleanup","cutover","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"PinkForge session 15: picking up. Creating dependency_eradication.rs with compile-time guards, forbidden import scanners, and migration report builder.","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:08:37Z"}],"assignee":"PinkForge","commit":"0594e2ba"}

 {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.8.3","title":"[asupersync][cutover] Canary rollout, rollback rehearsals, and fail-safe drills","description":"Execute staged canary rollout and rollback rehearsals for runtime-migrated paths under controlled fault conditions.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Define canary cohorts, promotion criteria, and rollback triggers for runtime cutover.\n2. Run rollback rehearsals that prove fast, safe recovery from failed rollout states.\n3. Unit tests validate rollout state-machine helpers and policy gates.\n4. Integration tests exercise promotion/rollback transitions in automation.\n5. E2E scripts run canary+rollback drills with exhaustive structured logs and artifacts.\n6. Enforce user-disruption budgets (latency/error/recovery thresholds) during canary promotion decisions.\n7. Heavy rehearsal and soak-like runs are executed via rch exec -- ... with reproducible evidence capture.","notes":"Rollout safety must be practiced, not assumed. Treat rehearsal evidence and user-impact budgets as release prerequisites. Require deterministic fixtures/seeds and replayable test evidence so failures are reproducible across environments.  {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.8.4","title":"[asupersync][cutover] Final go/no-go review and closure evidence package","description":"Assemble final technical decision package proving migration goals met and residual risks accepted/mitigated.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Produce consolidated evidence package covering tests, benchmarks, incidents, rollback rehearsals, and post-cutover soak outcomes.\n2. Include explicit crash/restart persistence evidence proving deterministic recovery and no silent user-state loss for critical workflows.\n3. Document unresolved risks, ownership, and follow-up controls explicitly.\n4. Unit/integration/e2e gate results are included with reproducible command artifacts.\n5. Final review checklist is machine-checkable and human-readable.\n6. Closure only when all prerequisite beads are closed and evidence completeness checks pass.","notes":"Future maintainers should be able to understand exactly why and how this migration was accepted without external context. Closure evidence must include structured logging outputs, diagnostic traces, and reproducible command artifacts, including rch-executed heavy validation runs and explicit user-impact outcomes for CLI, robot, watch, and session workflows. Require deterministic fixtures/seeds and replayable test evidence so failures are reproducible across environments. This bead must include comprehensive unit tests for scope-local logic, edge cases, and failure branches with deterministic fixtures and replayable seeds. This bead must include cross-module integration tests that validate boundary behavior, data contracts, and degraded/failure interactions under representative workloads. All diagnostics/logging/artifact outputs in this bead must comply with secret-safe redaction and privacy policy gates defined in ft-e34d9.10.7.5, with evidence captured in validation artifacts. Heavy compile/test/benchmark/soak workloads for this bead must run via rch exec -- <command>, with reproducible command records and artifact paths captured in completion evidence.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:26:56.181246Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-12T00:00:51.273376+00:00","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","cutover","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","release","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8.4","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8.4","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8.4","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8.4","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8.4","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8.4","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8.4","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8.4","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8.4","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.8.4","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.7","type":"blocks"  {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.8.5","title":"[asupersync][cutover] Post-cutover soak and user-journey confidence gate","description":"Run prolonged post-cutover soak and user-journey confidence validation to ensure stability under realistic sustained workloads before final closure.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Define soak matrix covering long-running ft watch, robot orchestration loops, session persistence, remote SSH flows, restart cycles, and mixed workload bursts.\n2. Execute sustained soak with failure injection and confirm no critical task leaks, deadlocks, message-loss, or unbounded latency regressions.\n3. Include crash/restart persistence verification scenarios (state durability and deterministic recovery for capture/session/search/control-plane workflows) as mandatory soak gates.\n4. Unit tests validate soak harness controls, anomaly detectors, and pass/fail classifiers.\n5. Integration tests verify automated rollback trigger logic derived from soak metrics and incident signals.\n6. E2E scripts run user-journey matrix with detailed structured logs, anomaly timelines, and reproducible artifact manifests.\n7. Heavy soak/test jobs are executed via rch exec -- <command> and command history is archived for audit.","notes":"Final cutover confidence requires long-horizon behavior proof, not just short CI passes.
